evals.prompt: How do I set up lazy loading with loadComponent in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Use { path: 'dashboard', loadComponent: () => import('./features/dashboard/dashboard.component').then(m => m.DashboardComponent) }. All feature routes MUST be lazy loaded. Use loadChildren for route files: loadChildren: () => import('./features/user/user.routes').

evals.prompt: How do I write a functional route guard in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Use CanActivateFn: export const authGuard: CanActivateFn = () => inject(AuthService).isAuthenticated() ? true : inject(Router).createUrlTree(['/login']). Register in routes: { canActivate: [authGuard] }. Class-based guards are deprecated.

evals.prompt: How do I use withComponentInputBinding for route params?
evals.expected_output: Add withComponentInputBinding() to provideRouter(routes, withComponentInputBinding()). Then define a signal input in the component: id = input.required<string>(). Angular auto-maps route params, query params, and resolve data to matching inputs.

evals.prompt: Working on app.routes.ts — how should I structure my routes?
evals.expected_output: Keep route definitions clean: no logic inside route config. Use guards for access control and resolvers for data pre-fetching. Lazy load all features with loadComponent or loadChildren. Use route data: {} for static metadata like titles.

evals.prompt: How do I use a resolver to prefetch data before navigation?
evals.expected_output: Create a resolve function: export const userResolver: ResolveFn<User> = (route) => inject(UserService).getUser(route.paramMap.get('id')). Register in routes: { resolve: { user: userResolver } }. Avoid blocking UI too long — prefer loading states in components for slow data.

evals.prompt: How do I configure a title strategy in Angular Router?
evals.expected_output: Provide a custom TitleStrategy service: @Injectable() class AppTitleStrategy extends TitleStrategy { updateTitle(snapshot) { ... } }. Or use simple route data: { title: 'Dashboard' } with the default TitleStrategy when withRouterConfig({ titleStrategy }) is configured.

should_not_trigger: How do I set up routing in React Router v6?
